creator: merlyn@geeks.org
text:
I also don't have IPv6 access to update2.freebsd.org from my work place

$ ping6 update2.freebsd.org 
PING6(56=40+8+8 bytes) 2001:4980:2:0:c24:afb7:2982:f829 --> 2620:11c:5001:1099:1337::117
^C

$ traceroute6 update2.freebsd.org 
traceroute6 to update2.freebsd.org (2620:11c:5001:1099:1337::117) from 2001:4980:2:0:c24:afb7:2982:f829, 64 hops max, 12 byte packets
 1  fwgw6.staff.iphouse.net  0.377 ms  0.255 ms  0.232 ms
 2  gw6.staff.iphouse.net  1.025 ms  1.259 ms  1.248 ms
 3  2001:4980:0:10::188  0.806 ms  0.809 ms  1.837 ms
 4  2001:4980:0:10::189  0.818 ms  1.101 ms  0.823 ms
 5  10gigabitethernet17.switch3.msp1.he.net  1.390 ms  1.316 ms  0.952 ms
 6  100ge11-2.core1.sea1.he.net  34.408 ms  51.252 ms  33.787 ms
 7  v6-six2.metapeer.com  34.056 ms  34.066 ms  34.369 ms
 8  2620:11c:5001:982::  34.787 ms  40.600 ms  37.135 ms
 9  * * *
^C

IPv4 connectivity to update2 works fine.
